brief.idea: Turn any repository into a strategic brief — five evidence-backed answers and a 3D product atlas explored from orbit down to the landmarks.
brief.problem: Evaluating someone else's product from its repo is slow: code tours explain how it works, never why it wins, and PMs end up reverse-engineering strategy from scattered files.
brief.why: It separates judgment from rendering: LLM agents may be fuzzy, but every claim they emit is validated against one contract, linked to evidence, and rendered deterministically — a brief that is playful on the surface and auditable underneath.
brief.how: LLM analyzer agents emit JSON fragments; a deterministic CLI validates them against one Zod contract and renders a self-contained dashboard; per-stream fingerprints keep re-runs incremental.
brief.takeaway: Worth it if you regularly size up unfamiliar repos and want the 'so what', not a code tour.

continents.title: Architecture
continents.summary: Every brick knows its place: LLM agents produce judgment at the edges, a deterministic core validates everything they say, and a React dashboard renders the result. Nothing crosses a layer except one JSON contract.

cities.name: Deterministic core
cities.summary: The trust boundary: validates, fingerprints, and assembles agent output.

landmarks.name: Schema validator
landmarks.detail: A Zod schema is the single contract; superRefine adds cross-reference rules and a non-fatal warning tier.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Keeps untrusted agent output from ever reaching the UI unchecked.

landmarks.name: Assembler CLI
landmarks.detail: Merges agent fragments into repo-brief.json and exits 0/1/2 so the orchestrator can react mechanically.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Turns fuzzy LLM output into a build step with a pass/fail signal.

cities.name: Dashboard
cities.summary: Renders the brief for humans — globe, outline, and prose share one view model.

landmarks.name: React dashboard
landmarks.detail: An interactive web view of the brief: strategic cards, the 3D atlas, and the competitive landscape.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Lowers onboarding effort to near zero.

landmarks.name: Pure view adapters
landmarks.detail: buildAtlasView turns the brief into deterministic lat/lng and an outline; WebGL never touches business logic.
landmarks.whyItMatters: The one seam that makes a 3D toy testable like a library.

continents.title: Modules
continents.summary: Four workspace packages and three agents, each a prefabricated piece with one job: schema, pipeline, dashboard, export — bolted together only through repo-brief.json.

cities.name: Workspace packages
cities.summary: npm workspaces with single responsibilities and explicit exports.

landmarks.name: @grasp/schema
landmarks.detail: Owns the BriefDoc contract, validation errors, and the warning tier.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Every other package imports the contract instead of guessing shapes.

landmarks.name: @grasp/export
landmarks.detail: Renders the brief to Markdown and a print-ready HTML page, escaping everything untrusted.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Makes the analysis shareable beyond the dashboard.

cities.name: Analyzer agents
cities.summary: Markdown-prompted sub-agents that return only JSON fragments.

landmarks.name: Essence analyzer
landmarks.detail: Reads README, docs, and core files to emit idea/problem/how plus the six-continent atlas.
landmarks.whyItMatters: The single richest fragment — the whole 'how it works' story flows from here.

landmarks.name: Landscape analyzer
landmarks.detail: Searches for alternatives and emits the competitive landscape graph.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Separates market judgment from product mechanics so neither pollutes the other.

continents.title: Workflows
continents.summary: Design once, assemble anywhere: a wizard sets depth, agents fan out in parallel, the assembler validates, and fingerprints make every later run incremental.

cities.name: Analysis run
cities.summary: From bare /grasp to an open dashboard in four phases.

landmarks.name: Depth wizard
landmarks.detail: Two axes — depth (docs/skim/deep) and broadness (offline/web) — gate how much code the agents read.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Lets one tool serve a two-minute skim and a deep teardown.

landmarks.name: Parallel dispatch
landmarks.detail: Essence, success, and landscape agents run concurrently and never share state.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Keeps wall-clock time flat as analysis streams are added.

cities.name: Incremental updates
cities.summary: Only stale streams are recomputed on a re-run.

landmarks.name: Fingerprint diff
landmarks.detail: sha256 fingerprints per stream decide what is stale; only changed streams re-dispatch.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Keeps re-runs cheap enough to run on every commit.

landmarks.name: Post-commit hook
landmarks.detail: An opt-in git hook flags stale streams after each commit and reminds you to re-run.
landmarks.whyItMatters: The brief stays honest as the code drifts.

continents.title: Business Flows
continents.summary: The value pyramid: a newcomer asks one question, explores the answer from orbit to landmark, then ships the brief to teammates as a report — understanding flows downhill from a single command.

cities.name: Newcomer onboarding
cities.summary: One command turns an unfamiliar repo into a guided answer.

landmarks.name: Strategic brief
landmarks.detail: Five answers — idea, problem, why it wins, how, takeaway — each backed by evidence chips.
landmarks.whyItMatters: A PM gets the 'so what' before any code is opened.

landmarks.name: Atlas exploration
landmarks.detail: The reader descends Orbit → Continent → City → Landmark, detail emerging per altitude.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Curiosity does the onboarding; nobody assigns reading.

cities.name: Sharing & export
cities.summary: The brief leaves the dashboard as paste-ready artifacts.

landmarks.name: Markdown report
landmarks.detail: report.md renders the atlas as an outline and the landscape as Mermaid — paste-ready for a README or PR.
landmarks.whyItMatters: The analysis travels to where the team already reads.

landmarks.name: Print-to-PDF page
landmarks.detail: report.html is a self-contained print page; headless Chrome turns it into a PDF.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Stakeholders without a terminal still get the full story.

continents.title: Technical Selection
continents.summary: Stones cut to fit this mountain: Zod because the contract is the architecture, JSON fragments because agents must stay isolated, WebGL because the map is the message, a static dist because briefs must open anywhere.

cities.name: Contract-first stack
cities.summary: Choices that keep LLM output trustworthy.

landmarks.name: Zod over JSON Schema
landmarks.detail: Runtime validation and TypeScript types from one source, with superRefine for cross-reference rules.
landmarks.whyItMatters: One definition serves compile time, runtime, and the docs.

landmarks.name: Fragments over shared memory
landmarks.detail: Agents exchange nothing but JSON files on disk; the assembler is the only merger.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Any agent can be re-run or replaced without touching the others.

cities.name: Rendering stack
cities.summary: Choices that make the output feel like a product.

landmarks.name: WebGL globe over 2D SVG
landmarks.detail: react-globe.gl gives camera flights and real continents; the cost is no globe in static export, accepted explicitly.
landmarks.whyItMatters: The altitude metaphor only lands when you can actually fly.

landmarks.name: Static dist over dev server
landmarks.detail: The dashboard ships as a self-contained folder that fetches one JSON file — no server, no build.
landmarks.whyItMatters: A brief is a file you can email, not a deployment.

continents.title: UI/UX Taste
continents.summary: Beauty as productivity: a playful travel metaphor carries a serious evidence discipline — every claim wears its source, and every pretty surface has an accessible twin.

landmarks.name: Altitude ladder
landmarks.detail: Four camera altitudes — Orbit, Continent, City, Landmark — are literally the data's nesting levels.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Zooming is understanding; the camera teaches the hierarchy.

landmarks.name: Landmark motifs
landmarks.detail: Six real monuments — Great Wall to Opera House — each chosen as a concept for its domain.
landmarks.whyItMatters: A memorable image per domain makes the teardown stick.

cities.name: Trustworthy by default
cities.summary: The fun never outranks the evidence.

landmarks.name: Evidence chips
landmarks.detail: Every claim in every panel links to a source chip — file reference or URL, verified or inferred.
landmarks.whyItMatters: Readers can always ask 'says who?' and get an answer.

landmarks.name: Outline fallback
landmarks.detail: Without WebGL the same data renders as an accessible nested outline; the list view doubles for screen readers.
landmarks.whyItMatters: The 3D flourish never gates the content.

nodes.name: Sourcegraph Cody
nodes.oneLiner: AI coding assistant with codebase context.
nodes.differentiator: Commercial, IDE-embedded, not a static graph.

evidence.claim: The assembler CLI validates fragments and exits 0/1/2
buildAtlasView is a pure, deterministic adapter
Analyzer agents return only JSON fragments
An opt-in post-commit hook flags stale streams
The atlas exports as a Markdown outline
report.html is a self-contained print page
A two-axis wizard sets depth and broadness
Camera altitudes drive level-of-detail on the globe
A Zod schema is the single contract between agents and UI
An accessible outline mirrors the globe
